"It might make sense just to get some in case it catches on."
- Satoshi Nakamoto, 2009

"The root problem with conventional currency is all the trust that's required to make it work. The central bank must be trusted not to debase the currency, but the history of fiat currencies is full of breaches of that trust."
- Satoshi Nakamoto

"Lost coins only make everyone else's coins worth slightly more. Think of it as a donation to everyone."
- Satoshi Nakamoto

"If you don't believe it or don't get it, I don't have the time to try to convince you, sorry."
- Satoshi Nakamoto

"I've been working on a new electronic cash system that's fully peer-to-peer, with no trusted third party."
- Satoshi Nakamoto, 2008

"I'm sure that in 20 years there will either be very large transaction volume or no volume."
- Satoshi Nakamoto

"The nature of Bitcoin is such that once version 0.1 was released, the core design was set in stone for the rest of its lifetime."
- Satoshi Nakamoto

"With e-currency based on cryptographic proof, without the need to trust a third party middleman, money can be secure and transactions effortless."
- Satoshi Nakamoto

"I would be surprised if 10 years from now we're not using electronic currency in some way, now that we know a way to do it that won't inevitably get dumbed down when the trusted third party gets cold feet."
- Satoshi Nakamoto

"WikiLeaks has kicked the hornet's nest, and the swarm is headed towards us."
- Satoshi Nakamoto, his second-to-last public post, 2010

"The Times 03/Jan/2009 Chancellor on brink of second bailout for banks."
- Satoshi Nakamoto, encoded in Bitcoin's Genesis Block

"Governments are good at cutting off the heads of a centrally controlled network, but pure P2P networks seem to be holding their own."
- Satoshi Nakamoto

"It's very attractive to the libertarian viewpoint if we can explain it properly. I'm better with code than with words though."
- Satoshi Nakamoto

"Proof-of-work has the nice property that it can be relayed through untrusted intermediaries."
- Satoshi Nakamoto

"We have to trust them with our privacy, trust them not to let identity thieves drain our accounts. Their massive overhead costs make micropayments impossible."
- Satoshi Nakamoto, on banks

"Bitcoin is a bank in cyberspace, run by incorruptible software, offering a global, affordable, simple, and secure savings account to billions of people that don't have the option or desire to run their own hedge fund."
- Michael Saylor

"Bitcoin is money. Everything else is credit."
- Michael Saylor

"You may not be interested in Bitcoin, but Bitcoin is interested in you."
- Michael Saylor

"The destiny of money is to be encrypted."
- Michael Saylor

"Bitcoin is digital gold - growing harder, smarter, faster, and stronger due to the relentless progression of technology."
- Michael Saylor

"All my best investments were in networks that everyone needed, no one could stop, and few understood. Bitcoin is the monetary network."
- Michael Saylor

"Like Bitcoin, living things are volatile. Dead things, not so much."
- Michael Saylor

"There are decades when nothing happens, and there are weeks when Bitcoin happens."
- Michael Saylor

"Bitcoin is the dominant digital monetary network. The next billion members will pay trillions to join. You might want to join first."
- Michael Saylor

"Bitcoin is an ark of encrypted energy to escape the currency flood."
- Michael Saylor

"Bitcoin is the most efficient system in the history of mankind for channeling energy through time and space."
- Michael Saylor

"21st century investors lost trillions clinging to 20th century ideas. Gold peaked in the 19th century. Buy Bitcoin."
- Michael Saylor

"Everyone gets Bitcoin at the price they deserve."
- Michael Saylor

"In the presence of rampant inflation, cash and credit become crumbling liabilities."
- Michael Saylor

"Bitcoin is a swarm of cyber hornets serving the goddess of wisdom, feeding on the fire of truth, exponentially growing ever smarter, faster, and stronger behind a wall of encrypted energy."
- Michael Saylor
The Bitcoin chart looks terrifying in weeks. It looks inevitable in years. Zoom is a strategy.
Bitcoin does not reward urgency. It rewards patience. Almost nothing else in finance works that way.
Every bear market in Bitcoin history ended. Every single one. The question is never if, only when.
Time is the only variable that has always worked in Bitcoin's favour. Add enough of it and the noise disappears.
In Bitcoin, the people who did the least, bought and forgot, have consistently outperformed the people who did the most.
There is no line in any government budget that reads 'we will stop printing money.' Bitcoin has that line in its code.
The 21 million limit is not a choice Satoshi made. It is the founding principle around which everything else was designed.
Every fiat currency in history has eventually returned to its intrinsic value: zero. Bitcoin was designed knowing that history.
Scarcity is the foundation of value. Bitcoin has the most provably scarce supply of any asset ever created.
Money printing is silent taxation. Bitcoin is the only savings vehicle that cannot be taxed this way.

Not your keys, not your coins. Three years after FTX, this is still the most important sentence in Bitcoin.

The Fear & Greed Index does not tell you what to do. It tells you what everyone else is doing, which is often the opposite of useful.
Markets are driven by emotion in the short term and by fundamentals in the long term. Bitcoin's fundamental is 21 million.

Every time the signal has been green and the price has been low, long-term holders have been the only buyers. They are also the only ones who got rich.

Every country that bans Bitcoin still has citizens using it. The demand does not respond to legislation.
The Austrian school of economics was right about sound money. Bitcoin is what sound money looks like in the 21st century.
Hard money, self-custody, long time horizons. These are not Bitcoin values. They are traditional values. Bitcoin just makes them digital.

Bitcoin is not trying to be a payments network, a smart contract platform, or a stablecoin. It is trying to be money. That focus is its strength.
Every altcoin is a variation on a theme. Bitcoin is the theme.
The market has tried to replace Bitcoin with something better 500 times. It has failed 500 times.
There is no second-best in monetary networks. History shows one money wins per era. Bitcoin is making its case.

Bitcoin's critics have been consistent. Bitcoin's price has been more consistent.
Nobody who held Bitcoin for 4 years has lost money from any entry point in history. Not one.

Most people will understand Bitcoin after it has already done most of its work. A few will understand it before.
